routes.name: Riders on the storm
routes.description: Stand start compression and work your way straight up

routes.name: Serendipity
routes.description: Sit-start on the left side of the boulder, going slightly left for the top-out.

routes.name: Fight or flight
routes.description: Start in the center of the face, on two small holds and power left to a pocket then through a wide hollow feature and then to a small dimple for a scary top-out. Watch out as holds might still break.

routes.name: Akonisma
routes.description: Start on the middle of the triangular face and climb up using the left diagonal arete and holds on the middle of the face.

routes.name: O Zografos
routes.description: Sit start on two crimps to eventually gain the pocket and get stood up on the slab finishing direct.

routes.name: Valanidi
routes.description: Starting on the big sloper/jug make somewhat big moves between good holds to gain the top. First move can be difficult for the short.

routes.name: Akida
routes.description: Starting on two crimps gain the spike and finish direct. A lovely technical crimpfest.

routes.name: Charcoal
routes.description: Get stood on the bloc from a low stand start and gain the good crimp rail above.

routes.name: Allin mas ediksan...
routes.description: Start low on a large protruding side-pull, move straight up until you reach the large in-cut rail and top-out same as "Charcoal".

routes.name: Fovies
routes.description: Sit Start using a slopey sidepull and the arête itself to climb up the overhanging arête, 6B from standing.

routes.name: Iptamenos Skiouros
routes.description: Make a running jump using the large ledge to gain the crimp rail above and top out. Fun!

routes.name: Superfunk
routes.description: Sit start at Zografos and move left to gain the arete finishing up Fovies.

routes.name: Efthinophobia
routes.description: Sit start at the slopey jug of “Zografos” and traverse right to link up with “Valanidi” following the obvious line of sideways crimps.

routes.name: Akida (sds)
routes.description: Sit-start of 'Akida' adding two moves before getting established like the stand start

routes.name: Black Diamond
routes.description: Start like 'Alin mas ediksan...' but then veer right with a series of undercuts until you make a big move to the arête and top out here. Quite morpho and careful as the fall-zone is not ideal

routes.name: Iptamenos Vatrahos
routes.description: Elimination dyno. Step onto the big ledge using two underclings and jump and latch the crimp rail above like ‘Iptamenos Skiouros’

routes.name: Sick Leave
routes.description: Sit-start at "Valanidi", move out left to gain the pocket of "Zografos" and top-out there.

routes.name: O Pellos Zografos
routes.description: Start on ‘Zografos’ but direct mantle with a hook left, no additional holes on the slab. (A lot of pressure on the right wrist)

routes.name: Avarice
routes.description: Start like ‘Superfunk’ but instead of going out left to the arête go direct to gain a small crimp and a sharp pocket before going out left to the arête and top out like ‘Superfunk’

routes.name: Aerial Duet
routes.description: Stand on the ledge and dyno to the jug left to then dyno again out left to the lowest lip of the boulder

routes.name: Eis Diploun
routes.description: Start on the ledge and dyno to the jug left like “Aerial Duet” but then do a direct dyno to the highest point of the boulder

routes.name: Charcoal (sds)
routes.description: Start from seated start at the side pull crimp beneath the ledge into “Charcoal”

routes.name: Riddle me this
routes.description: Sit start at the corner with side pulls and make tricky moves to get to the slab and top out

routes.name: Bane
routes.description: Sit start same as Riddle me this but follow the arete to the left and top out

routes.name: Gotham
routes.description: Stand start and work your way up and to the right to top out

routes.name: Robin
routes.description: Stand start on sidepulls and make a dynamic move to the slopey hole and top out on the slab

routes.name: Lovecraft
routes.description: Start with a good LH jug and RH crimp to reach the lip and mantle on the slab.

routes.name: Poison Ivy
routes.description: Sit start and climb up a diagonal rail to reach another juggy diagonal above to mantle on the slab.

routes.name: Two Face
routes.description: Sit start same as “riddle me this” but stay low and traverse all the way left to finish up “Poison Ivy”.

routes.name: Kalamionas
routes.description: Start sitting at the far right around the arete and traverse diagonally left following the rising lip to its maximum height, then top out on the slab.

routes.name: Hush
routes.description: Stand start and make your way up on slowpy crimps

routes.name: Eyes of the Spider
routes.description: Start in the middle of the boulder, just about where a small overlap starts to form, and traverse right and up through the small overlap. Tricky technical start to get established on the face.

routes.name: Spiderboy
routes.description: SS where a crack/flake forms following it until the overlap and then reach over for the finish.

routes.name: Spider Web
routes.description: SS trending left and exit on the left slab. Overlapping with “Eyes of the spider" and tops out further left.

routes.name: Fangs of the Spider
routes.description: Extension to 'Eyes of the Spider.' Start on the black coloured jug and traverse along to meet 'Eyes of the Spider'

routes.name: Pipe-weed
routes.description: Start on the two good holds in the back of the cave, moving straight out, slightly left for the top-out.

routes.name: Black-headed bunting
routes.description: Start on a RH sloper and make your way up through small feet (without using the ledge on the right) and make a few committing moves to the top.

routes.name: Feathered Fissure
routes.description: Start on crimps/pockets and make a few long moves to gain the crack and climb through it.

routes.name: Boba Fett
routes.description: Start one hand in the undercling and the other hand in the pockets

routes.name: Cad Bane
routes.description: Start matched on the tufa-like hold

routes.name: Jango Fett
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Mind over mat
routes.description: Interesting moves in an overhang start, followed by a slab with good pockets. At least 3 mats are recommended.

routes.name: Mind over mat (sds)
routes.description: The sit-start definitely adds to the difficulty of the route, as it requires flexibility and reach.

routes.name: Free ride
routes.description: The start is a bit crimpy, but the top has nice jugs.

routes.name: Rustry
routes.description: Straightforward climb in the middle of the boulder. Can be a bit mental for someone new to bouldering.

routes.name: Mrs. Beaver
routes.description: Sit-start in a slight overhang with good holds to the top

routes.name: Dreams of flight
routes.description: Interesting start with hand jams in the crack (easier than trying to lean back) and somewhat stretched moves to reach the good holds afterwards.

routes.name: Take flight
routes.description: Sideways dyno starting on the small boulder on the left and dynoing up right to grab the sloper edge.

routes.name: Line across the sky
routes.description: (Scramble up above the canyon to find this boulder with a nice view) sit start on good holds and traverse left using the under face too if needed

routes.name: Helly donny
routes.description: Sit start on the small fallen boulder and use the crimp slots to come out

routes.name: Deimos
routes.description: A beautiful highball that goes through the longest line of the face. Start out left on a slightly overhanging face on the large sidepull and finger slot, make a few powerful moves to climb up and right and finish up the crack.

routes.name: Fovos
routes.description: Another highball starting out right on a sloper to break left making a big cross move to the pinch topping out as for Deimos. More balancy than powerful.

routes.name: Shot in the dark
routes.description: Start on a low side pull and a thin crimp to get off the ground and move between good holds to get over the bulge and onto the slab.

routes.name: Dark matter
routes.description: Sit-start on the diagonal rail to gain the pocket and get established on the large flake and top-out.

routes.name: Don’t tell mum
routes.description: Difficult start leads to easier slab climbing

routes.name: Typewriter
routes.description: Beautiful highball with really pleasant movement

routes.name: Louvithkia
routes.description: Start up the ledge and use the crack to gain the good sloper topping out on the slab face. chossy

routes.name: Eskotothin
routes.description: Start on good holds to gain the arete and top out as for Louvithkia. Care for loose holds

routes.name: Matter
routes.description: Start like 'Shot in the dark' and traverse all the way right to the far end and top out

routes.name: Antimatter
routes.description: Start far right matched in the big hole and traverse left all the way to 'Shot in the Dark' and top up there

routes.name: Enaeria Prosklisi
routes.description: Start to the left of 'Typewriter' and climb directly using a series of pockets, sidepulls and crimps to gain the good holds to top out.

routes.name: Prism
routes.description: A very nice line starting with a couple of tricky moves and then a pleasant but still engaging ride to the top, following a crack.

routes.name: To Diedro
routes.description: Climb the obvious dihedral/corner using both sides.

routes.name: Zeimbekiko
routes.description: An excellent climb, climb up the slab to dyno to the juggy hollow feature before making a commiting move in order to gain the top. The upper slab does still have some lichen and could do with a clean.

routes.name: Haramada
routes.description: The crack problem on the right of the dihedral problem produces some nice layback moves.

routes.name: Omorfa
routes.description: Another problem on the middle of the face, nice flowy moves on good holds.

routes.name: Lio Paratzi
routes.description: Stand start on any of the many options and gain the jug finishing up this.

routes.name: The Shield
routes.description: Start standing and pull onto the large shield feature climbing up this via good holds.

routes.name: Moon Landing
routes.description: Start on the undercut to gain the two small pocket features using these to fly for the flat sloper out and left.

routes.name: Aspida
routes.description: Start on the left near the arete and traverse out right to gain the undercuts and top-out same as “The Shield”.

routes.name: Haramada (sds)
routes.description: Start sited and make a couple of fingery moves to gain the crack.

routes.name: Hyla
routes.description: Stand start using the arête to scale to the top. Quite mossy so be careful when topping out.

routes.name: Sunset shot
routes.description: Climb the arete/corner using a series of good pockets.

routes.name: Sirtaki
routes.description: Start on the slab below the left side of the overlap. Climb up without using the crack on the left to gain the good scope above and topout direct.

routes.name: Fun times with friends
routes.description: Climb the middle of the slab direct on slopers, without using the left crack/groove or the large holds on the right.

routes.name: Fun times solo
routes.description: Start on the slab below the distinct groove and get established in the groove, to climb it up through good holds.

routes.name: Serpent's Head
routes.description: Start left hand on crimp and right hand on edge hold and traverse your way up the boulder topping out at it's full length (using only this boulder).

routes.name: Serpent's Fang
routes.description: Standing, mount the boulder without using the surrounding rocks/boulders

routes.name: Stathou
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Stathou allo lio
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Perpata
routes.description: Traverse start at 'Stathou', get your heel up and off you go

routes.name: Erpysi
routes.description: Climb up to a large pocket and crawl between the tree branches top-out.

routes.name: Corner Head SS
routes.description: LH slopy side pull and RH slopy crimp. Large pocket over You head is eliminated. Enjoy the start (short people: low start)

routes.name: The Neural Network
routes.description: Climb any of the possible options, all about 3 font grade.. The distinctive slab with beautiful brain-like features is a great option for a beginner boulderer wanting to get a bit higher with ease. Try one-arm or no-hand combos to make it more interesting.

routes.name: Synapses
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Hypothalamus
routes.description: The leftmost side of the boulder (also the potential downclimb)

routes.name: Cerebral Cortex
routes.description: The middle line

routes.name: Temporal Lobe
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Escape
routes.description: Sit-start in the little cave on the left and climb out and straight up.

routes.name: Slap
routes.description: Sit-start to gain the RH slopey pinch and slap to the good sloper on the left and go up. 6A+ from standing.

routes.name: Lapdance
routes.description: Climb up the groove on the left of the overlap, reach the two good pockets and exit using holds on the face. Committing at the top.

routes.name: Todo Recto
routes.description: Climb up the overlap direct to a committing mantle up the face. Thread lightly with the large hold on the lip.

routes.name: Round and About
routes.description: Go up and right around the overlap via the good holds on the flake to gain the top.

routes.name: Crouching Tiger
routes.description: Sit-start to gain the undercut and eventually the good pocket higher up and finish direct. 6A+ from standing. Avoid going too much on the right.

routes.name: The Bonnie Situation
routes.description: Sit start similar to crouching tiger and make your way right and up to a good finish

routes.name: Pnasta
routes.description: Starting on two low edges, smear on very small dimples to powerfully charge out left on edges before commanding easier ground higher up with a few step-up moves.

routes.name: O Erimitis
routes.description: Same start on the holds of "Pnasta" on the left, but instead of charging up left, use the gentle groove on the right and holds around it to top out rightwards.

routes.name: Ta mathkia tou Kkoulla
routes.description: Use a low side-undercling, to explode up to a large pocket. Top out straight up through some precarious moves. Do not underestimate the finish and avoid going around.

routes.name: Ta mathkia tou Kkoulla RHS
routes.description: Same start to the direct version, but once gaining the left cave/pocket, traverse right to the right cave/“eye”, before moving up through some technical moves to the top.

routes.name: Lone child
routes.description: Start on the left side of the boulder but avoiding the arete, stand on the platform until you reach the horizontal crack where the climbing begins.

routes.name: Warm-up for the family
routes.description: Start low on the arete and work your way up. Can be done as a sit-start to add a bit to this short line.

routes.name: Mossy feet
routes.description: Watch out for the moss on some footholds.

routes.name: Bravo re anipsin
routes.description: Use some pockets, small edges and mostly your feet to go up this one.

routes.name: Family Tree
routes.description: Begin at 'Family Feud' and traverse all the way right to top out at 'Blood and Water'

routes.name: Family Feud
routes.description: Furthest left problem on the boulder. Climb with with care as mossy and difficult to spot due to brambles

routes.name: Bravo anipsia mu
routes.description: Follow the obvious crack

routes.name: Through Thick and Thin
routes.description: Similar to 'Family Feud' but steers right for an easier top out

routes.name: Blood and Water
routes.description: Furthest to the right boulder problem. Make your way up and left to the hole/crack and mantle up.

routes.name: Extended Family
routes.description: Sit start of 'Blood and Water'

routes.name: Practically Napping
routes.description: A short easy traverse along the edge of the rock.

routes.name: Skysalts
routes.description: Sit start on low holds in the black crack feature. A short technical ascent where the use of a heal is recommended.

routes.name: Forced Awake
routes.description: A sit start variant of "Practically Napping", finish at the same point and eliminate top rail.

routes.name: Intrepid Shenanigans
routes.description: Start from the crimps above the bowl-like feature

routes.name: Intrapit Shenanigans
routes.description: Sit start of Intrepid Shenanigans. Left hand starts from the bowl feature and right hand on any of the crimps.

routes.name: Traversée de Eli
routes.description: Traverse and top out over the bowl feature

routes.name: Traversée de Felix
routes.description: Traverse and top out onto the slab, before the pit below the boulder

routes.name: Straight Outta Drouseia
routes.description: Stand start with any combination of pocket, crimp or side-pull crimp to reach a small positive tooth and topout straight up.

routes.name: Anthropopoulli
routes.description: Climbs the bulge feature. Stand start with a left hand crimp and a sidepull (both around shoulder height). Can also be done as a sit-start which doesn't add any difficulty.

routes.name: Oi re inda skalui
routes.description: Sit start on the large platform to reach and mantle the big ledge/jug out right, and top out straight. Not hard but committing due to the less than ideal landing.

routes.name: Lolola Patatou
routes.description: Start with a left hand crimp and right hand pinch and make one hard rock-over move to a good brown scoop to top out straight up.

routes.name: Bonafide
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: King Charles's Crown Mantle
routes.description: Start sited at the juggy hole below the prow, reach the slopey ledge above and mantle to the top.

routes.name: Jaws
routes.description: Start laying down (back firm on the ground) at the lowest two holds and compress your way up both sides. Fun little compression!

routes.name: Flirting with sharks
routes.description: Start sited at the juggy hole and traverse the lip and around the corner to top out same as "Jaws".

routes.name: The Fin
routes.description: Start on edges and make a single rock-over move to get established on the face, grab the "Fin" and topout on the right.

routes.name: Lightweight
routes.description: Start both hands on the large juggy hole, put your feet up and make a big move out right to the sidepulls in the crack and top out leftwards with a bit of difficulty.

routes.name: Pumping Iron
routes.description: SS on the positive dish and go up to eventually gain the good sidepull flakes and top out as for Lightweight.

routes.name: Roid Rage
routes.description: Sit-start on the good flat hold to gain the good rectangular edge above, then top out with some difficulty.

routes.name: Do you even lift bro
routes.description: Sit-start on the far left of the boulder and climb straight up.

routes.name: Mr. Olympia
routes.description: Sit-start LH on the starting crimp of “Pumping Iron” and RH low on a sloper and climb rightwards up to a dyno for the jug on the break above and top-out the tricky slab towards the right.

routes.name: Push and Pull
routes.description: Sit-start on the good flat hold and sidepull (same as “Roid Rage”) to gain the good rectangular edge above, but then move right towards the faint crack/groove for a harder but nicer slab finish.

routes.name: Never skip leg day
routes.description: Stand start and make a few tricky foot moves to eventually reach a good slopey horizontal hold up high to top-out the slab.

routes.name: Creatine
routes.description: Sit-start at the good in-cut hold of "Pumping Iron" and make some powerful moves to reach the big hole on the left, to top out same as "Lightweight".

routes.name: Mr. Olympia (stand)
routes.description: The standing start to “Mr. Olympia”. Dyno to the jug and top out the slab.

routes.name: Crossfit (stand)
routes.description: Stand-start on the right side of the ledge, using the big sideways pockets, go straight up into crimps and mantle direct

routes.name: Crossfit (sds)
routes.description: Sit start like ‘Mr. Olympia’ and go straight up to meet the stand

routes.name: Buffalo Bill
routes.description: Really interesting route with slab moves on a 20 degree overhang.

routes.name: Hello Clarice
routes.description: Warm-up route on nice holds and predictable moves.

routes.name: Silence of the lambs
routes.description: Crimpy, balance moves on a slight overhang with the hardest moves near the top.

routes.name: Firefighter
routes.description: Very nice compression problem. Sit-start on two positive holds and climb up using both aretes.

routes.name: Pyrosvestiras
routes.description: Climb on the right of the arete using holds on the face, without veering too right.

routes.name: Ashes
routes.description: Climb a series of edges to the top.

routes.name: Fire ladder
routes.description: Climb some diagonal “zic-zac” cracks keeping on the left of some wedged blocks. Beware for some loose rock.

routes.name: Kaminada
routes.description: Climb the obvious chimney as an off-width (i.e. wedging the whole body in with your back against the wall). Fun but contrived.

routes.name: Inferno
routes.description: Start on a good edge and climb up past thin side-pulls/gastons to a tricky finish. Classic.

routes.name: Fireman's traverse
routes.description: Start on the far-right of the block and using the high holds for hands on the obvious shelf, traverse left all the way to the exit of “Inferno”.

routes.name: Dante
routes.description: Sit-start to "Inferno" which adds a hard crimpy pull off the ground.

routes.name: Phoenix
routes.description: Start at the arete and use the raily crimp to traverse to the good hold on ‘Inferno’ and finish up this.

routes.name: Cody
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Whack-a-Cody
routes.description: Sit start to ‘Cody’ starting left in a compression

routes.name: May
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: May Goodwin
routes.description: Sit start to 'May'

routes.name: Rose’s Room
routes.description: Stand start to the right of 'May'

routes.name: Balcony
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Ramen
routes.description: Climb the short arête from a sit start.

routes.name: Moon Baboon
routes.description: Stand start matched on the good hold just above head height and make a big move and some technical moves before finishing up the slab face

routes.name: Rancor Pit
routes.description: Sit-start in the left-hand corner and go straight up.

routes.name: Darth-Finger
routes.description: Stand-start on the side-pull left of the corner of the main face, going straight up on a pocket and crimps.

routes.name: Hands Solo
routes.description: Sit-start on the right side of the main face, in a slight overhang and work your way up on good jugs.

routes.name: Boba Feet
routes.description: Short traverse from the right side of the boulder, going left, and finishing in the corner.

routes.name: The Rancor
routes.description: Sit start in the left corner of the boulder, going diagonally left on jugs and crimps holds.

routes.name: Jabba the Hutt
routes.description: Sit start on the far left of the ledge and traverse to join ‘Darth-Finger’

routes.name: Tiure’s Temple
routes.description: Boulder located at the top of Jabba the Hutt. Cool little feature

routes.name: Jedi mind tricks
routes.description: Climb the corner from standing, starting in a press position mid-height and stemming/bridging to the top.

routes.name: Jedi mind tricks (sds)
routes.description: Sit start on the left side of the corner, to get established into the corner, and climb up stemming and bridging to the top.

routes.name: Darth Maul
routes.description: Sit start on two crimps to the left of the arête (same as "Rancor") and climb direct to the top via some flowy dynamic moves.

routes.name: Eglepe
routes.description: Climb the highball slab at its maximum height. Make a few committing moves past slopey edges and tricky feet at mid height, to gain good holds higher up. Take care as it still needs some cleaning from moss.

routes.name: Good Samaritan
routes.description: A highball slab with a committing top-out on good holds. Take care as it still needs some cleaning from moss.

routes.name: Groovy
routes.description: Climb the distinct groove.

routes.name: Mambo
routes.description: Make sure you have a spotter to not fall on the tree underneath

routes.name: Chiquibonbon
routes.description: Sit start the base of the arete on the right and climb up using the arete and face.

routes.name: Jumbo Prawn
routes.description: Sit start on the little ledge and climb through pockets.

routes.name: Kourambies
routes.description: Hard sit start on crimps leads to easier ground through edges and pockets.

routes.name: Prawn cocktail
routes.description: Sit start on the larger ledges and make your way up.

routes.name: Bombos
routes.description: Climb the diagonal compression line from a powerful sit start.

routes.name: Pombastic
routes.description: Sit start on the left on compressing on two edges (same as Pombos) and climb right on the face.

routes.name: Appiitouri
routes.description: A fun contrived problem. Start on a large hollow feature on the left of the face and dyno for a large ledge and finish straight up.

routes.name: The Cracken
routes.description: Start on edges and a large hollow feature and climb past two diagonal cracks for a tall finish.

routes.name: Safari
routes.description: Sit start to reach the diagonal rail/crack leaning left and following it up to the top.

routes.name: Movement Deactivated
routes.description: Sit start and rise on the crater looking slab until you reach the bulge and top out on this.

routes.name: Activated
routes.description: Sit start with two hands in the crack

routes.name: Movement Activated
routes.description: *Eliminate* Sit start like ‘Movement Deactivated’ and make your way left using only the lowest horizontal crack line until reaching the end of said line on the far left

routes.name: Prologos
routes.description: Tricky start leads to some technical movement higher up

routes.name: Attunement
routes.description: Start on the ledge and climb direct without veering right to the good crack holds

routes.name: Lasani
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Beluga
routes.description: Start on the ledge and climb direct to reach the beluga whale

routes.name: Ton nou sou sto skaloui
routes.description: Start on the ledge and climb direct using a series of cracks before topping out

routes.name: Kataklida
routes.description: Start on the ledge and make your way up using some technical feet to gain the hole

routes.name: Fossa
routes.description: Follow the crack line to the top

routes.name: Meerkat
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Frappe
routes.description: Sit-start on the left of the small cave and climb up and slightly rightwards avoiding the arete.

routes.name: Sunny-side up
routes.description: Start on the right side of the small cave and climb up to gain a good hold at the top for the finish.

routes.name: Crack-ed egg
routes.description: Where a thin crack is formed, start with a high right hand as a side-pull and power up some nice edges to the top. Very nice!

routes.name: Barouti gia progevma
routes.description: Start on two high and sharp crimps, lock-off and explode for a positive hold higher up before finishing straight up.

routes.name: Barouti gia progevma (sds)
routes.description: A sit-start variation of "Barouti gia progevma", starting down left make a big cross move to a sharp crimp in order to join the standing version, finishing up this.

routes.name: Corner Café
routes.description: Very nice problem climbing the steep corner sit-starting on a low side-pull jug.

routes.name: Noris Noris
routes.description: The first problem on the north face starting from the left. Sit-start low on jugs and make a cross-move to a good flat edge on the left and then further left to a perfect scoopy hold before topping straight up.

routes.name: Varin Progevma
routes.description: Excellent problem starting low using a good undercling/sidepull and moving up towards a large hollow feature before veering slightly right for a committing top-out. The top requires some cleaning but the problem climbs well.

routes.name: Gero Ksipnima
routes.description: Sit-start low below a small overlap and climb straight up, without using the large holds of "Kalimera" on the right.

routes.name: Kalimera
routes.description: A nice flowy climb. Same start as "Gero Ksipnima" but traversing right on jugs to gain a positive spike high right. Rock over and top out the boulder through the groove.

routes.name: Kalimera direct
routes.description: Sit-start matched on the lowest jug, below the small overlap and power up through positive holds to finish off in the groove like "Kalimera".

routes.name: Easy morning
routes.description: The first short line on the left. Use any combination of holds to climb up. Can be used to get to "Krimmenos" boulder at the back once you top-out.

routes.name: Jesse Pinkman
routes.description: Start on a good sidepull for your right hand and move up to an undercut, trending left through some better holds to reach the top.

routes.name: Walter White
routes.description: Start as for Jesse Pinkman but instead of trending left through better holds move up direct to gain a small but positive right hand crimp before going for the top. Trickier for the short.

routes.name: Eat yo eggs
routes.description: Sit-start between “Sunny-side up” and “Crack-ed egg”. There are a few options for holds. There's a variation using the large jugs in the cave on the left, joining "Sunny-side up" for a nice 6A+.

routes.name: Kalimera j'efeksen
routes.description: Excellent link-up of two problems. Sit-start same as “Corner Café” and traverse left to join the good edge/jug on “Barouti gia progevma”.

routes.name: Barouti ston kafe sou
routes.description: Another great and harder link-up. Sit-start at “barouti gia progevma”, gain the positive jug and join right on the undercling of “Corner Café” to top it out through the corner.

routes.name: Baby Blue
routes.description: Sit-start same as “Noris Noris” on large flakes in the small cave and climb straight up to top-out same as “Jesse Pinkman”.

routes.name: Morning Glory
routes.description: Right to left low traverse of the north face. Start at Kalimera (direct) and traverse all the way left to top-out same as “Noris Noris”.

routes.name: Rise and Shine
routes.description: Another link-up to 'Barouti gia Progevma.' This time after getting the main jug go left with a reachy shouldery move to finish up 'Cracked Egg'

routes.name: Efta ofta afka
routes.description: A harder extension to "Kalimera j'efeksen" beginning the same but veering off left from the good edge/jug to finish like "Rise and Shine/Cracked Egg"

routes.name: Beans on toast
routes.description: Start left on the flakey hold and right hand on the crimp and dyno up right to the jug and top out

routes.name: Full Monty
routes.description: Start matched on the flakey side pull and dyno all the way up to the jug (the middle one of the three)

routes.name: Brunch
routes.description: Sit start and follow the arete until you reach the corner to topout there.

routes.name: Escargot likes to boulder
routes.description: Start on the pocket for your RH and climb up and left to the top.

routes.name: Escargot Central
routes.description: Climb up the centre of the face starting on two pockets.

routes.name: Escargot Flakey
routes.description: Start on a good flake, pull on and reach a good pocket to top-out straight up with the help of the arete.

routes.name: Snail walk
routes.description: Starting on a good hold move up and use the arete to top out.

routes.name: Snail Arete
routes.description: SS the far right of the boulder to climb the lip trending leftwards to join the arete and finish up this.

routes.name: Escargot likes to sit
routes.description: Sit-start to gain the good flakes/pocket and top-out same as “Escargot  likes to boulder”

routes.name: Escargot Power
routes.description: Sit-start on a large pocket feature and climb up through pockets and edges to top-out same as "Escargot Central"

routes.name: Snail with wings
routes.description: SS low on a slopey and large pocket to reach the good hold on the start of “Snail walk” and power to the lip.

routes.name: Snail Trail
routes.description: Traverse most of the face topping out the obvious crack

routes.name: Snail Trail (Feet Only Variation)
routes.description: Same as 'Snail Trail' but without using hands (including the top out)

routes.name: Mishellin Star
routes.description: Sit start matched in the crack

routes.name: Kolos tou Karaolou
routes.description: Sit start using the plethora of different options to make your way up

routes.name: Shoumalisma
routes.description: Start sitted on the furthest right point and traverse low to reach the jugs of Mishellin star and top out.

routes.name: Titsirokaraolos
routes.description: Start low on the left side of the boulder and traverse all the way to the right at the start of shoumailisman. Top out at kolos tou karaolou.

routes.name: Gouda
routes.description: Sit start and follow the crack line all the way up

routes.name: Kefalotyri (sds)
routes.description: Sit start with the LH on the comfortable side pull and using any of the pockets or side pulls to get to ‘Kefalotyri’

routes.name: Kefalotyri
routes.description: Using a series of pockets and side pulls get to the victory jug and top out here

routes.name: Blue Cheese
routes.description: Sit start on the big flake and follow the diagonal flake to the top. Mind the mould!

routes.name: Just Jammin
routes.description: Sit start at the base of the crack and follow this up and exit right as to avoid the thorn bush.

routes.name: Styx
routes.description: Sit start at the base of the overhanging prow using a mix of compression and heel hooks to gain the top.

routes.name: King Flare
routes.description: Stand start LH on a crimp and RH on a side pull

routes.name: Durin
routes.description: Start sited in the faint corner and get established in the flakey crack. Make somewhat big moves through good holds and make a final committing move to the lip. Such a nice flow and one of the best at this grade!

routes.name: Ghollum's sit
routes.description: Hard sit-start on crimps/edges (on the right side of "Durin" just before the path goes steep towards the river) leads to easier flowy climbing on the arete-like feature that joins the finish of "Durin". Be careful not to roll down the river otherwise you might turn into a Ghollum!

routes.name: Balrog
routes.description: Stand start on the two obvious crimps and traverse right to join “Durin”and finish up this

routes.name: Three degrees of separation
routes.description: Climb the arête from a sit-start low, climbing from its steep side (RHS). Some cleaning may be required but the main useful holds are clean.

routes.name: Loose Bowel
routes.description: Sit start in the mini-cave with a LH sidepull jug and a RH pocket jug. Make a couple of powerful moves to rock over onto the slab.

routes.name: Slot machine
routes.description: Sit start with one hand in the sidepull jug and one in the slot crimp hold

routes.name: Cacao
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Manger
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Fissure
routes.description: The crack from standing.

routes.name: Nice and Easy
routes.description: Climb straight up using good holds.

routes.name: Cleaning crew
routes.description: climb up via some edges before getting to better holds above.

routes.name: To Xorafi
routes.description: Climb up via good holds

routes.name: Samcro
routes.description: Sit Start on two crimps to move up along the left arete without using the good large hold to the left of the arete itself.

routes.name: Kra kra
routes.description: A shorter problem that sit-starts on any of the undercling/sidepull options to eventually reach a good top hold to topout.

routes.name: V.L.C
routes.description: Start sitting on a large undercling and move up and leftwards through better holds.

routes.name: Vyzastra
routes.description: Start sitting at a large undercling and move up and rightwards.

routes.name: Hug a Spurs fan
routes.description: Climb the pillar shape, compressing your way up from a sit-start.

routes.name: Korazinos
routes.description: Sit start on small edges (at "Samcro") to gain an undercling edge, and then reach the good jug out to top out there.

routes.name: The Wave X
routes.description: The leftmost easy problem

routes.name: The Wave Y
routes.description: The middle problem

routes.name: The Wave
routes.description: Best line on the Bloc, move through the slight overhang using the giant flakes.

routes.name: Kimatothraustis
routes.description: Start right hand side pull/undercling and make your way up the obvious crack

routes.name: Windbreaker
routes.description: Go up the arête on the slab facing side

routes.name: Rogmi
routes.description: Start left at a round feature/jug, reaching out right to a diagonal crack to top-out using that.

routes.name: Krimmenos Misharos
routes.description: Starting on a right hand on a jug and a left hand low left, reach out left to a triple-pocket hold, before finishing up the crack.

routes.name: Ksipetsismenos Kourkoutas
routes.description: Sit start to reach out to the side-pulls and make your way up the crack.

routes.name: Den einai afto pou nomizeis
routes.description: Climb up the slab through crimps.

routes.name: P-arete-ame
routes.description: Climb the slab on the right side starting on the faint arête.

routes.name: That's the shot re Silvio!
routes.description: Climb the layback crack starting seated on its left-side.

routes.name: Putschnitzel
routes.description: Start sitting to the right of the diagonal crack. Use some underclings/side-pulls to establish on the face, to then reach out left and finish up the crack.

routes.name: Ghetto Gekko
routes.description: Sit start on the far left, traverse right way through slopers and crimps to reach the 3 finger pockets. From there move slightly lower and continue right, exiting via the crack on the far right.

routes.name: Άδε Την Σαύρα
routes.description: Sit start as for Ghetto Gekko making the same moves to reach the crack. Instead of going right, move to the undercling on the left and follow the crack to the top.

routes.name: O Kyklopas
routes.description: Start on a small pinch crimp and move left to gain the large hole mantling into this and climbing out!

routes.name: The Groove
routes.description: Climb the pocketed groove, this is also the downclimb

routes.name: Piroulos
routes.description: Climb up using positive features to gain the top.

routes.name: Ponirada
routes.description: Start as for Kyklopas but move right to a sloper using the pinches on the side of the large hole but without recourse into the hole itself.

routes.name: Hollow Feeling
routes.description: Climb the precariously hollow flake finishing slightly to the left, extra care needed as the flake feels like it may break!!

routes.name: Flakey Slab
routes.description: Climb up the slab using good holds all the way.

routes.name: Flakes for days
routes.description: Climb up the slab using good holds all the way.

routes.name: Eyes on the prize
routes.description: Start in the hollow feature and work your way up to a good hold at ¾ height.

routes.name: Polyfimos
routes.description: Climb up the obvious line of pocket to make a tricky move before gaining good holds just before the top.

routes.name: Ammata
routes.description: Get established on the face on the right of where "Piroulos" starts and traverse left to gain the hole and top-out same as "Kyklopas"

routes.name: Minyas
routes.description: Start matched on the ear flake and go straight up

routes.name: Ornytiwn
routes.description: Using side pulls and underclings to get up

routes.name: Thersandros
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Almos
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Glafkos
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Sinwn
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Vellerofontis
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Porfyriwn
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Pocket Rocket
routes.description: Start standing on two positive holds to move towards the centre of boulder gaining two good pockets and finishing direct.

routes.name: Pocket Rocket (sds)
routes.description: SS using a good undercut to make a big pull to get established on the start of Pocket Rocket, finishing up this.

routes.name: Easy Peasy
routes.description: The easy slab on the right side of the boulder.

routes.name: The Arete
routes.description: Climb the right arete using a good hidden hold near the top to assist with the top out.

routes.name: The Bulge LH
routes.description: Sit Start beneath the bulge matched on a large crimp to gain the good dish on the bulge and finishing left.

routes.name: The Bulge RH
routes.description: Start beneath the bulge matched on a large crimp to gain the good dish on the bulge finishing right.

routes.name: ERP1
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: ERP 2
routes.description: Tricky mantle.

routes.name: O Kipouros
routes.description: Start on two positive undercuts to gain the good crimp rail on the lip above.

routes.name: O Kipouros (sds)
routes.description: Sit start to the right of Kipouros and make a couple of moves to gain the undercuts and the lip above.

routes.name: Mantila
routes.description: Either climb the diagonal flake or use the good crimps to gain the lip to the right, mantling up this to finish.

routes.name: ERP 3
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Seam-ingly Good
routes.description: Climb the diagonal seam of good holds to the left of the prow.

routes.name: The Prow
routes.description: Start on the layered pinch and spikey jug to gain two sidepulls on the prow finishing direct.

routes.name: Toto CY
routes.description: SS on two slopey rails to gain the good slopey jug above and make a couple of technical moves to the better holds above.

routes.name: Layin Low
routes.description: SS on the right side of the boulder and traverse the lower holds to the left to finish up the prow.

routes.name: Rachokokkalia
routes.description: SS as for the Prow but move right to gain the good sloper jug and then reach for the large spikey jug, finishing to the right.

routes.name: The Bulge Central
routes.description: An eliminate. SS beneath the bulge matched on a good crimp and climb straight up through slopers/edges, staying central without using the large pocket on the right.

routes.name: The Bulge Traverse
routes.description: Start on the far right of the bulge and traverse left staying low, avoiding the large pocket-hold on the “Bulge RH”.

routes.name: Nyhterida
routes.description: Dyno from a crimp to the lip

routes.name: Regga
routes.description: Start on the left of the groove/corner after the overhang, below a protruding bulge and climb up and  on top of it.

routes.name: A Way Out
routes.description: Start matched on the ledge near the hole and traverse right staying low using a series of crimps and good feet until you reach the same holds ‘The Bulge LH’ and exit here

routes.name: Reousa Kinisi
routes.description: Sit start. Line furthest to the right

routes.name: Low Rider
routes.description: SS matched in the large undercut in the mini-roof then gain a good hold in the black streak out right, finishing up.

routes.name: Low Rider Right
routes.description: Start the same as for Low rider but this time break out right to good holds to  finish up the right arete.

routes.name: Hydroponic
routes.description: SS with RH on the larger pillar/column and LH in a slopey pocket to break out right for the good horn shaped hold finishing up.

routes.name: The Heel Deal
routes.description: SS on the smaller pillar, RH on a good crimp above and use some heel trickery to get off the ground to gain better holds up and to the right finishing up.

routes.name: The Contortionist
routes.description: SS on a good pocket and somehow get standing on the ledge to reach the small but good pocket higher up.

routes.name: Opos to Melin
routes.description: Climb the right-trending crack to the top. The best intro to highball bouldering you could have. All holds are sweet as honey.

routes.name: Thkio Ppalies
routes.description: Balance your way up the easy-angled slab to reach the pocket hold and top out straight up.

routes.name: Pani
routes.description: Start left and end right and top out

routes.name: Koupi
routes.description: Traverse right to left and top out

routes.name: Phineas
routes.description: Traverse from left to right started on the big juggy horn hold (the opposite boulder is not in)

routes.name: Argw
routes.description: Start on the hand moulded hold and traverse left and top out (the opposite boulder is not in)

routes.name: Mossila
routes.description: Starting on two small but positive crimps at about head height, make big moves between crimps to gain the top.

routes.name: Torquemada
routes.description: SS on a shallow undercut LH and Sloper RH, make powerful moves through better holds to gain the top.

routes.name: Masquerada
routes.description: Variation of “Torquemada”, exiting right (easier) once gaining the spike as a left gaston.

routes.name: Salience
routes.description: Sit start with RH on the undercling and LH high up on the left crimp hold and finish up 'Mossila'

routes.name: Tiny Titan
routes.description: Sit start on a sidepull flake/jug to reach the ledge out left and mantle.

routes.name: Bulbasaur
routes.description: Climb up using the arête compression climbing to the top

routes.name: Mi-ledge
routes.description: Start on crimps before launching onto a great ledge

routes.name: Get that mileage
routes.description: Sit start to ‘Miledge.’ Adds a couple of tricky and powerful moves before getting established like the stand start

routes.name: Lavdano
routes.description: Sit start hands matched in the obvious crack hold

routes.name: Rodo
routes.description: An eliminate but great moves. Sit start like ‘Get that Mileage’ and establish like the stand start but instead of going for the big ledge traverse right (without using the big ledge) until you link to the big crack and finish like ‘Lavdano’

routes.name: Ivysaur
routes.description: Sit start and using the lowest holds slap your way up to the stand and finish up ‘Bulbasaur’

routes.name: Escape from Hoth
routes.description: Sit start and climb up compressing to the top.

routes.name: Rebel's Rest
routes.description: Sit start on pockets and make a few snatches to the top.

routes.name: Hyperdrive Hustle
routes.description: Sit start on side pulls to reach out to the slopey lip and top out.

routes.name: From Romania with Love
routes.description: Sit-start to eventually gain the jug on the lip of the short overhang

routes.name: Balmos
routes.description: Sit start on the far right of the boulder and make your way up direct with a sloppey top out. (Careful of the hole)

routes.name: Sarmale
routes.description: Sit start with the LH on the flake and RH in the pocket higher up right

routes.name: Jumari
routes.description: Start like ‘Balmos’ and traverse left and top out where the crack forms

routes.name: Tatooine
routes.description: Sit start compressing at the arete and topout on the slab.

routes.name: Tatooine flatbread
routes.description: Stand start on the right of the arete and climb up the slab.

routes.name: Tatooine sunset cocktail
routes.description: Stand start on the left of the crack and climb up the face/slab.

routes.name: Tatooine sunset cocktail (sds)
routes.description: Sit start on the left of the crack and climb up the slab face.

routes.name: Nuna Roast
routes.description: Sit start at the base of the crack and climb up to top out on the slab.

routes.name: Bounty Hunter (sds)
routes.description: Sit start on the rock, below the bulge on underclings/sidepulls in the crack and make a few strenuous moves to the top.

routes.name: Bounty Hunter
routes.description: Stand start and climb up through slopey jugs.

routes.name: Yoda Stew
routes.description: Start standing on the left of the arete and traverse to the far right staying low.

routes.name: Moogan Tea
routes.description: The small bloc at the back. Traverse using the top of the boulder from right to far left.

routes.name: Rage against the machine
routes.description: Sit start with LH undercling and RH in a pockety feature and make big moves up to mantle on the shelf

routes.name: Bulls on parade
routes.description: Start LH undercut and right hand on a sloppey pocket and make your way up and over the shelf

routes.name: Headbanger
routes.description: Start with the undercling and make a reachy move to the jug flake before doing a dyno to the lip

routes.name: Riff
routes.description: Start matched on the undercling and make your way to the right

routes.name: Whammy bar
routes.description: Start matched in the undercling and make your way left to reach the stand

routes.name: Nosebleed section
routes.description: Using a series of side pulls/flakes and crimps climb direct without veering to the pockets on the left

routes.name: Whammy bar (stand start)
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Guerilla radio
routes.description: Stand start on good jugs on the left of the ledge

routes.name: Matouthkia
routes.description: Sit start using two eye-shaped little pockets

routes.name: Lounge
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Nosebleed seat A
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Nosebleed seat B
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Nosebleed seat C
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Nosebleed seat D
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Orc-a way up
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Echolocation
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Tantrum
routes.description: Stand start on the slopey sidepull RH and the small LH crimp high up to make some tricky and reachy moves to the top.

routes.name: Bermuda triangle
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Xalara
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Technokratis
routes.description: The best line on the face, follows a series of pockets and small breaks/cracks to exit leftwards.

routes.name: Karvouno
routes.description: Same start as "Technokratis" but exits rightwards to ease the difficulties.

routes.name: Hidden Dragon
routes.description: Start using the undercut for your left and a crimp for your right to gain the pockets and then, the sloper above before breaking left to the hidden pocket, going for the top from there.

routes.name: The Corner
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Flying Daggers
routes.description: Climb up through side-pulls to make a somewhat committing move to a good hold before topping out with ease.

routes.name: Fan of Knives
routes.description: Start on the undercut LH and gaston RH to gain the flat flake beyond the mini roof and top-out.

routes.name: The Shelf
routes.description: Climb out of the right side of the cave, using the large features.

routes.name: Gravel Pit
routes.description: Sit Start matched on the  slopey undercut to move up through the overhang.

routes.name: Liquid Swords
routes.description: Sit-start to follow the undercut flake up until making a couple of tricky moves to mantle onto the slab finishing up this.

routes.name: 36 Chambers
routes.description: Start on the good undercut and a slopey crimp to make a big move up to a good pocket topping out as for tantrum.

routes.name: Tantrum (sds)
routes.description: Sit-start on the right to join up the standing start of Tantrum and top it out.

routes.name: Baby Dragon
routes.description: Start same as “Hidden Dragon” but top-out straight up through slopers to gain the good flake (instead of veering left to the hidden pocket).

routes.name: Roaring Dragon
routes.description: Sit-start right on good holds and make powerful moves to join and finish up “Hidden Dragon”. Alternatively finish up “Baby Dragon” for a 6B+.

routes.name: THC & Taurine
routes.description: Sit Start with RH on the juggy sidepull, LH on the slopey crimp to climb up and right to the juggy ledge matching this before dropping off.

routes.name: Katana
routes.description: Sit start and make some hard moves on small features to establish on the slab face

routes.name: Pandora
routes.description: A techy line between "Tantrum" and "THC & Taurine". Climb the face through a series of crimps, pockets and feet trickery to reach a jug out left and top-out.

routes.name: Teardrop
routes.description: Wallrun to the obvious juggy ledge. Very fun!

routes.name: Sweet candy
routes.description:
Added by
Demetris Papakyriacou

routes.name: No candy for me
routes.description: Make some technical and relatively long moves on the face without using the good jugs on the left.

routes.name: Lime Squash
routes.description: Get established on the side-pull flakes and make some nice layback moves to gain the top ledge.

routes.name: CBD & Caffeine
routes.description: Stand start to the right of 'THC & Taurine' with the RH in on the undercut and the LH up high gaston to make some physical moves to attain the glory jug and top here

routes.name: Tantrum Direct
routes.description: Start like ‘Tantrum’ but instead of exiting left after grabbing the good hole jug make a dynamic move to a sloper for a direct finish

routes.name: Full on tantrum
routes.description: Tantrum sit start into Tantrum direct

routes.name: Castle in the sky
routes.description: Starting with high a LH jug and RH pinch, make some technical compression moves to the top.

routes.name: Castle in the sky
routes.description: Start sitting in the groove to gain the jug and finish compresing up the face.

routes.name: Guardian of the sky
routes.description: Start standing to gain the diagonal seam, traversing leftwards following the seam to the top of “Castle in the sky”

routes.name: Stay humble eh
routes.description: Climb up through the bulge and topout the slab

routes.name: Ponyo
routes.description: Sit start at the base of the slab and work your way straight up to the ledge and continue straight up

routes.name: Whisper of the heart
routes.description: Stand start

routes.name: Psallidaki
routes.description: First problem on the right of the east face - start on a low side pull and pull on good crimps up to a good side pull and to the top.

routes.name: Mallin
routes.description: sit start on a good large flake and explode up left to a thin crack gaston. Then move slightly left and upwards to an easier finish.

routes.name: Eipa sou koureftou lion
routes.description: sit start on the left side using a good left flake and a right hand on a thin side pull on the right about the same height. Pull up hard along the crack to a good flake and work your way up to rightward finish.

routes.name: Plagiasmos
routes.description: Start on the left corner of the southwest face (facing Lara bay). Use a large foothold-platform low left, a right hand pocket and a left hand gaston/sloper to get established on the face. Then top-out through a series of pockets/gastons.

routes.name: Pepe
routes.description: Stand start on slab wall to reach for big ledge and mantle over to good jugs.

routes.name: Trihas
routes.description: Start sitting on rock and get right heel hook up on the ledge the both hands matched on the obvious jug.

routes.name: Kkelis
routes.description: Sit-start same way as "Trihas" but veer right and then top out.

routes.name: The Clinch
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Slab X
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Slab Y
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Slab Z
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Desert Shaheen
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Eleutheros Skopeftis
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Chasing Sunsets
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: The Arete
routes.description: Climb the right arete using a hidden jug near the top to top out.

routes.name: Anything goes
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Corn Flakes
routes.description: Very flakey. Watch the holds as they are not very secure!

routes.name: Persefone
routes.description: Sit start at the protruding bulge below the small roof with a LH pocket and a RH rectangular edge, gain two pockets on the roof and exit slightly right.

routes.name: Apagogi
routes.description: A roof traverse, starting same as “Persefone” and moving out and left to gain the arete and topout.

routes.name: Underworld
routes.description: Start sited with hands low on the arete and climb it up to finish on the scoopy jug same as “Apagogi”.

routes.name: Overworld
routes.description: Sit start at the bottom of the arete and exit with a mantle on the vertical face.

routes.name: Daughter of Zeus
routes.description: Sit start at the obvious base to top-out direct.

routes.name: Layby
routes.description: Start sitting by the step features and make a big reach to a jug on the slab.

routes.name: Kano pezodromio
routes.description: A mantle problem starting on a RH high undercling and a LH sloper to execute a mantle onto the slab.

routes.name: Eshei diavasi re gare
routes.description:
Added by
Demetris Papakyriacou

routes.name: Parkaro pasto pezodromio
routes.description: Balancy climb on the left side of the slab using smalle edges/dimples

routes.name: Poundo pezodromio
routes.description: Climb the slab on the right side of the face starting on a good ledge and moving slightly right and up.

routes.name: Monodromos
routes.description: Start sitting on the far right and traverse left following the line of slopers.

routes.name: Fystiki
routes.description: Start matched on the ledge and make your way up right

routes.name: Karydi
routes.description: Start matched on the crack and go up direct

routes.name: Pikramygdalo
routes.description: Start left hand in the pocket and right hand on the crimp

routes.name: Inquisition
routes.description: Start using holds in the large pocket feature and go up

routes.name: Crusade
routes.description: Stand Start and use good edges to gain the top.

routes.name: Pilgrimage
routes.description: SS on a pocket and a good edge to gain the Pope's Hat at the top.

routes.name: Excommunication
routes.description:
Added by
Christos Sophocleous

routes.name: Cardinal
routes.description: SS on good holds to climb the inclined prow to the top.

routes.name: Pope Lowe
routes.description: Start as for Inquisition and traverse to the right to finish up Excommunication.

routes.name: Habemus Papam
routes.description: SS using the crack to make an explosive move to land with a gaston on the slab face

routes.name: Oremus
routes.description: SS with LH in the spikey side pull and RH pinching down below on an obvious good hold

routes.name: Conclave
routes.description: SS inside the mini cave using whatever holds take your fancy and exit up onto the slab face

routes.name: Pulp Friction
routes.description: Sit start, use the right hand slopers and left hand crimp to get to the top without using the better holds right of the slopey arete.

routes.name: Slap Chris Rock
routes.description: Sit start same as ‘Pulp’ but continue right to get the good holds and finish up the slab

routes.name: Trou de Rat
routes.description: Start matched in the big ‘rat hole’. Morpho. Harder for the tall

routes.name: Skinner
routes.description: Start matched on the ledge

routes.name: Ratatouille
routes.description: Start with left hand on the arête and right on the low crimp

routes.name: Ride the Bull
routes.description: Stand start. Careful with falling on the rock underneath and generally needs a good head and the fall zone is less than ideal

routes.name: Flash or Splash
routes.description: Start on the edge of the little river pool, both hands on a jug and reach out to the arete to climb it up to its top.

routes.name: Froggy
routes.description: Tricky sit start through sidepulls/gastons, to reach the good flake.

routes.name: Amoivada
routes.description: Short problem, starting sited and climbing to the top through jugs.

routes.name: Meropi
routes.description: Stand start using the flakes and the arête and make your way with caution due to loose holds and lack of traffic

routes.name: Sisyphus
routes.description: Start matched on the rail and make your way up topping up the slab. Be careful of loose holds at the top

routes.name: Eliwti
routes.description: Start with the right hand in the crimp and the left in the pocket above

routes.name: Nubbin
routes.description: Start matched on the undercling. Top out can be a bit scary so have plenty of mats to conver the rock underneath

routes.name: Poundin trypa
routes.description: Start as “Elioti” on a slopey rail and climb left to reach a pocket and finish up with good holds.

routes.name: Birds of paradise
routes.description: Stand start with hands matched on the obvious hold , make big moves to jugs going straight up . Definitely much harder for the short

routes.name: Bougainvillea spectabilis
routes.description: Start as for birds of paradise but after the first move up go right to the hole/jug and top out right above the crack .

routes.name: Schism
routes.description: Make your way up the crack and make a somewhat big move left to the good jug and top out straight up .

routes.name: Schism (stand)
routes.description: Start standing and work your way up the crack and make a big move left to the good jug and top out with care straight up .

routes.name: Hyacinthus
routes.description: Start standing and make your way up on good holds .

routes.name: Mimosa (stand)
routes.description: Start on the large flakes/jugs below the overhang and make some powerful moves up and left before topping out up the slab in the groove

routes.name: Mimosa
routes.description: Start sited and make some powerful moves before topping out the technical slab. Keeps you engaged from start to finish.

routes.name: Ip Man
routes.description: Start left hand on the jug/handle at the entrance of the tunnel and make big moves to come out and left with some difficulty (especially not to dab)

routes.name: Mimosa ext.
routes.description: Start standing at the far right and traverse left using the tufa like pinches/flakes towards mimosa and finish up this . Take care at the top out !

routes.name: Follow the light
routes.description: Sit start at the entrance to the tunnel/cave and make few hard moves to get to the top

routes.name: Thyme
routes.description: Stand start on the side pull crimps and make your way straight up and once reaching the jug go right for the top out of birds of paradise

routes.name: Glory Hole
routes.description: Climb the longest line on the overhang through a series of large pockets and flakes. Be careful of hold breakage due to soft rock and not much traffic.

routes.name: Shadow of Doubt
routes.description: Sit start with LH undercling and RH on the good side pull and make tricky and powerful moves to join the stand start and finish up this

routes.name: Maple
routes.description: Start side pulls, make way up to rail crimp with big move, power endurance traverse with smears to birds of paradise!

routes.name: Lumos
routes.description: Start sitted on the block on the left of the entrance to the cave. Traverse along the lip of the cave to join and exit at “Follow the Light” at the far right.

routes.name: Glowing Edge
routes.description: Sit start on the rock with feet below the protruding overlap, stand up with underclings and make your way through smears to reach the arete.

routes.name: Karate
routes.description: Start on the juggy rail and make a powerful move to a slot/side pull to easier ground.

routes.name: Karate (sds)
routes.description: Start on a RH crimp gaston and a good edge for the LH to reach the starting rail of Karate and climb the stand.

routes.name: Thalis Experience
routes.description: Start in the large hole and make some nice moves to reach left and then back right to topout on good holds.

routes.name: Tunnel vision
routes.description: Start matched on the pockets and make moves in the roof to link into “Follow the light” and finish up this(no dabbing)

routes.name: Africa
routes.description: Stand start with LH on the side pull and the RH on the side pull crack and do a series of technical moves to get to the ‘Africa’ like hold and top out left of this

routes.name: Africa (sds)
routes.description: Sit start in the hole and link to ‘Africa’

routes.name: Psilocybin
routes.description: Start compressing on left side of the arete, climb up to a spike on the arete mid-height, then exit on the right side of the arete to top out on the slab

routes.name: Pachamama
routes.description: Sit start on the far right of the overhang and climb the compression line (using the crack and arete). Come in left to the top bulge for the top out.

routes.name: Mama Africa
routes.description:
Added by
Demetris Papakyriacou

routes.name: Glowing Face
routes.description: A technical/balancy variation of "Glowing Edge" which starts with the underclings below the bulge and once gaining the sidepull, moves out left to the face without the arete.

routes.name: MI6
routes.description: Start with left hand in a side-pull finger crack and right hand dimple. Climb up the crack/corner.

routes.name: Tip Toe
routes.description: Balance your way up small edges on the slab, without using the arete or the large protruding bulge on the left.

routes.name: Secret Santa
routes.description: Start deep inside the chimney at the bottom and make your way up the back (do not come out on the arete) to reach the top between the two blocs.

routes.name: The spy who loved me
routes.description: Using the arête and some crimps make your way up and finish on a sloppy mantle
Pleasant climb that makes use of the crack and holds around it.

routes.name: Wall ramp run
routes.description: As the name suggests wall run and grab the jug at the top . Fun
Lower start of “005” starting lower in the chimney/pit and following the crack to the start of “005”

routes.name: Disguised Face
routes.description: Climb the wall using holds just on the face (without using the crack on the left or the protruding block on the right - holds outside the crack are in).  An eliminate but climbs well!

routes.name: Ambush
routes.description: Climb up the protruding bulge using feet both on the wall below and beside it.

routes.name: Clockwork
routes.description: Stand start using both arêtes and make your way up

routes.name: Tourlou tourlou
routes.description: Start sited below the lip of the mini-roof and climb out and right. Stand-start goes at 6A+. The low sit-start at the back of the roof is a hard project.

routes.name: Imam Bayildi
routes.description: Climb the left side of the arete from a standing start.

routes.name: Papoutsakia
routes.description: Start on the flake. Easier for tall people

routes.name: Mantle me
routes.description: Sit start using the protruding sloper crimps and mantle

routes.name: Mantle me too
routes.description: Sit start with the pocket and crimp and mantle

routes.name: Arete
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Rift
routes.description: Stand start or French start reaching for the big undercut crack (try to not yank too hard) and climb direct with a series of side pulls to top out slightly right

routes.name: Slice & Dice
routes.description: Get established with use of a palm press and heel, reach to the sharp big pocket hold. The grade is given without French starting

routes.name: Corner Café no.2
routes.description: Sit start and climb up the crack feature

routes.name: Carpaccio
routes.description: Stand start using two small crimpers and go diagonally right

routes.name: Tartare
routes.description: Sit start and make a few powerful moves to then do straightforward top out

routes.name: Bigsterz
routes.description: Left most line. Sit start and rock over to the slab face and finish on this

routes.name: Sliver
routes.description: Stand start

routes.name: Slab Branch 3
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Slab Branch 2
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: Slab Branch 1
routes.description:
Added by
Paris Hadjisoteriou

routes.name: The Montessori teacher
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Braddock
routes.description:
Added by
Vasiliki Anastasiou

routes.name: Kirie Mavrokefale
routes.description: Sit start on the protruding bulge and mantle onto the slab.

routes.name: Chewie
routes.description: Sit start on the rock below the overhang and LH on a good slot to reach up to the lip and mantle onto the slab.

routes.name: Darth Vader
routes.description: Sit start on the far right of the little overhang (at "Anakin Slabwalker") and climb leftwards to follow the rising lip to its furthest point before topping out onto the slab.

routes.name: Anakin Slabwalker
routes.description: Sit start on the furthest right and lowest edges/flakes and make a few moves upwards and slightly left to reach the slab and mantle.

routes.name: C-3PO
routes.description: The first slab line on the left. Climb up to the first shelf and then direct to the top (can be a little mossy at parts)

routes.name: R2-D2
routes.description: Just to the right of ‘C-3PO.’ Climbs direct without veering to the big diagonal ledge to the right

routes.name: Ahsoka
routes.description: Climb the centre and main line of the slab face. Tip toe up the diagonal ledge and make some technical and committing moves to reach the highest part of the lip and top out

routes.name: Flowers of dark side
routes.description: The line on the far right using a series of small crimps until you get use the arête to help reach the top of the lip and top out (quite mossy at parts)

routes.name: Ancalagon
routes.description: Start on the protruding feature underneath the roof to climb out to the right.

routes.name: Fafnir
routes.description: Sit start on the left of the lip below the roof to move right before using the crack to top out above the overhanging prow.

routes.name: Ydra
routes.description: SS on the hanging feature on the right of the roof and climb out and then left, to gain the large crack and eventually top out at the top of the overhanging prow at its full length.

routes.name: Sigurd
routes.description: Extension to Fafnir, sit start at the back of the cave on the large undercling feature, to join Fafnir and finish up this.

routes.name: Ryujin
routes.description: Starting as for Fafnir traverse the entire overhanging lip to the right to finish up Ancalagon.

routes.name: Bahamut
routes.description: Start with hands matched on the positive undercut in the back of the roof to make some tricky moves in order to join ‘Ydra’ and finish up this. First 7C in CY!

routes.name: Leviathan
routes.description: SS on the hanging feature on the right of the roof (as for Ydra) but traverse left reversing the initial moves of Bahamut to join and finish up Nidhogg in the groove.

routes.name: Nidhogg
routes.description: Sit start starting matched on the good holds in the roof to climb out and finish toward the left side of the boulder, topping out in the groove.

routes.name: Ryujin Extension
routes.description: Extension to Ryujin, start as Sigurd at the back of the cave and once gaining the pinch on the lip below the roof, traverse right to finish like Ryujin.

routes.name: Ouroboros
routes.description: Another link-up, start as for Ydra but this time join and finish up ‘Sigurd’

routes.name: Balerion
routes.description: SS at the back of the cave and climb out and left on good holds but instead of exiting in the groove/corner, keep traversing right along the lip for a mantle finish.

routes.name: Hug a dragon
routes.description: Start on the protruding "tooth" to compress your way through and over the roof using both sides, to top out on the right. A bit "forced"/contrived but some nice movement.

routes.name: Susanoo
routes.description: Start as for Ydra and move left through the cave to join and finish Ruyjin extension. Somewhat contrived line but an obvious link-up.

routes.name: Scottie Too Hottie
routes.description: Start as for ‘Ydra’ but exit the boulder to the right as for Ryujin.

routes.name: Scottie doesn’t know
routes.description: Start deep in the cave like ‘Bahamut’ and join the starting hold of ‘Ydra’ and finish like ‘Scottie Too Hottie’.

routes.name: Amaterasu
routes.description: Yet another extension. Do ‘Susano’ and instead of finishing on the side continue up and left to the highest point of the boulder for a mantle finish.

routes.name: Sta sagonia tou drakou
routes.description: Start at the back left of the cave on the “ball” jug of “Nidhogg”, climb out left to exit in on vertical/slabby terain.

routes.name: Torque Wrench
routes.description: Stand start on the left of the arete using a RH sidepull feature and LH crimp and climb up.

routes.name: Torque Resistance
routes.description: Sit start using a good RH side-pull/jug and a LH microcrimp to gain the slopey sidepull feature and finish straight up.

routes.name: Easy Froggy
routes.description: Stand start positive holds to reach the large pocket hole and top out.

routes.name: Peat Ballet
routes.description: A tricky balancy slab, starting on small crimps to reach better holds for the top out.

routes.name: Bog Hugger
routes.description: Climb the arete from standing.

routes.name: Holy Step
routes.description: Start using the large hole to reach out left to jugs and top out.

routes.name: Aristodemus
routes.description: Climb the easy vertical/slab to the top.

routes.name: Leonidas
routes.description: Climb up following the crack and using holds around it to get the top.

routes.name: Gorgo
routes.description: Climb the face through edges and pockets to the top.

routes.name: A wee dyno
routes.description: Sit start matched on the obvious juggy hold and dyno straight up (mind your head) to the protruding jug. Really fun!

routes.name: Monkeying around
routes.description: Sit start mathced on the good hold and make a big move out right then traverse the lip to top out on  left

routes.name: Hand ladder
routes.description: Sit start to the left and work your way up the shelves and jugs

routes.name: Banana mission
routes.description: Sit start on the obvious jug and traverse left to end up hand ladder

routes.name: Mood fixed
routes.description: Sit start on the jugs and go left and then right for the jug above the lip and top out with a tricky mantle

routes.name: The Rib
routes.description: Really nice problem starting under the rib and moving through good holds to mantle onto the slab finishing up this.

routes.name: Yperionas
routes.description: Start on the first line of "shelves" and traverse rightwards around the corner and link-up to finish up "The Rib".

routes.name: Titanomahia
routes.description: Sit-start below a protruding prow and climb straight up.

routes.name: Kronos
routes.description: Sit-start on the shelf feature and climb through pockets and side-pulls to reach the large pocket and then top out rightwards.

routes.name: Tartaros
routes.description: Climb the center of the tall face on small edges and trusting your feet. Highball.

routes.name: Ouranos
routes.description: Stand start on good holds and climb directly through the bulge, compressing through a series of crimps, slopers and flakes to a committing finish.

routes.name: Banana Split
routes.description: Sit start on the lowest part of the crack and follow the crack line upwards

routes.name: Apple Crumble
routes.description: Sit start and make your way over the bulge to top out

routes.name: Crème Brûlée
routes.description: Sit start, using the many options make your way up the side of the boulder

routes.name: Pistoliá
routes.description: Stand start and make a few long moves to a mantle finish.

routes.name: Pistoliá (sds)
routes.description: Difficult sit start to get established with a couple of power moves to the top.

routes.name: Detox
routes.description: Sit start below a diagonal juggy rail and reach the slopey ledge to mantle same as "Pistolia".

routes.name: Slapstick
routes.description: Fun boulder problem (sit start), with an easy top, if you can find the right beta.

routes.name: Nitty-gritty
routes.description: Sit-start with good holds.

routes.name: Face-lift
routes.description: Stand-start with decent holds, some a bit slopey.

routes.name: Say hello to my little friend
routes.description: Start matched on the big pocket hold and dyno up to a little crimp directly above and mantle

routes.name: Doubloons
routes.description: Sit start with LH on the good jug just under the lip and RH on the pocket and make your way over the lip with a fun mantle

routes.name: Blackbeard’s Revenge
routes.description: Sit start with LH on the pocket used for ‘Doubloons’ and RH in an undercut pocket to make your way over the lip

routes.name: Bilge Rat
routes.description: Sit start low in the bilges using a LH on the sloppey undercut and RH on the crimp on the outside face of the cave and make your way to finish like the other problems

routes.name: The Arete
routes.description: SS at the base of the right to climb this to the top.

routes.name: Shipethkia
routes.description: Start on the obvious pockets to climb direct to the top, a little bit chossy where the rock is coloured black.

routes.name: Kontostouppis
routes.description: Tricky sit start low on a pocket for the right hand and some small edges/dimples for the left hand. Explode upwards for an undercling/gaston and mantle up.

routes.name: Mitsi alla rotsi
routes.description: Same start as "Kontostouppis" but instead of finishing direct, traverse right on the lip of the roof to reach an obvious sidepull flake and finish up and right.

routes.name: Shismathkia
routes.description: Start below the little roof and climb left using a crack as undercling and layback to gain better holds to top-out. Quite burly. Starting from the good undercling at the edge of the roof is about 6A+/6B.

routes.name: Gorilla
routes.description: Same start as "Shishmathkia" below the roof, but this time move out of the roof using holds on the roof and reach out for edges on the face to top-out.

routes.name: Anthropos Arahni
routes.description: Start with hands matched on a small crimps/dimples, reach out right before getting the arete out left to then move back right along the arete to top-out on the "triangle".

routes.name: Efcharisto
routes.description: Start on jug on right, top out along the slab

routes.name: Long hair don't care
routes.description: Start on jug on far left, traverse to the top out of Gorilla in the middle of the boulder

routes.name: Gorilla’s Heel
